reasoning: Query asks for shukubo (temple lodging). Top hits include irrelevant Chinese-language ryokan pages, Saga prefecture intros, and only one truly relevant Nagano shukubo article. The single useful entry (Tamashoin / Zenkoji) is buried behind noise; lang=en parameter ignored, returning Chinese descriptions.
missing_data: Famous shukubo such as Koyasan temple lodgings, Eiheiji, Yoshino — none surfaced in top results.
wrong_data: Lang filter not honored: descriptions are zh-CN/zh-TW even though lang=en requested.
improvement_hint: Filter results by language, deprioritize boilerplate cookie/privacy text, and add a 'shukubo' tag/category so temple-stay listings rank above generic onsen ryokan.

reasoning: Query asks about quiet onsen towns where visitors stroll in yukata. Result list is dominated by 'Onsen Shrine' Wikidata entries — same-named shrines, not towns. Real yukata-stroll towns (Kinosaki, Kurokawa, Ginzan) aren't surfaced. Data is grounded but ranked unhelpfully.
missing_data: Iconic yukata-stroll onsen towns: Kinosaki, Kurokawa, Ginzan, Shibu, Nozawa.
improvement_hint: Add a 'hot-spring resort town' classification distinct from shrine name matches; boost municipality-level onsen entries containing keywords like 浴衣/外湯めぐり.

reasoning: French query asking about whale-watching. Returned a Nagoya whale-statue, Monet's garden, Kyoto rivers, Miyazaki tourism boilerplate — none about whale watching. wrong_data: Result quality: most top hits are unrelated to whales beyond keyword match.
improvement_hint: Treat 'クジラ' as activity intent → cross-link to whale-watching DMO experiences; honor lang=fr or fall back to en.

reasoning: User wants deep tea-ceremony experiences in Kyoto. Tool returned Kyoto Japan Heritage stories — first hit '800-Year Walk of Japanese Tea' (Yamashiro) is on-target. But it includes off-topic Chinjufu story (Yokosuka/Kure/Sasebo/Maizuru) which isn't Kyoto. Useful as background but lacks bookable tea-house/temple programs.

improvement_hint: Tighten prefecture filter so only Kyoto-related stories surface; add cross-link from heritage to specific tea-experience operators.

reasoning: User wants mountain villages with infrequent train service. Query 'ローカル' returned generic 'About this page', planning sites, Hokkaido tourism portals — only Tsugaru DMO chunk mentions local rail. No actual route data, schedules, or named villages.
missing_data: Specific lines: Iiyama, Tadami, Koumi, Kisuki, Sanko; villages along them with low service.

reasoning: Returns 507 MAFF GI items — comprehensive and well-grounded. But intent is 'lesser-known' traditional food; top hits are Tajima/Kobe beef, Yubari melon — global-famous items. No filter for obscurity. Useful but agent must filter.
missing_data: A 'mainstream score' or popularity filter to surface less-known items first.
improvement_hint: Add a popularity/mainstream-tier metric so the agent can request unknown specialties; expose a sort param.

reasoning: Japanese version of L4-01. Same MAFF GI corpus, lang=ja honored properly. Same ranking issue — famous items dominate, lesser-known intent unmet without agent-side filtering.
missing_data: Obscurity / popularity scoring to push lesser-known items up.
improvement_hint: Same as L4-01: add popularity tier or a 'less-mainstream' query parameter.

reasoning: 183 traditional arts items via Bunka/Wikidata. Solid grounding with designation labels. But query is 'forgotten craftsman skills' — Nogaku and major folk festivals dominate top results, which are well-known. Endangered/rare crafts not prioritized.
missing_data: Endangered craft signal: number of practitioners, at-risk status. METI traditional crafts could be cross-merged.

reasoning: Query is Hokusai-print-like landscapes. Search '富士' returned Mt Fuji, Sengen shrines, Fuji-Q (amusement park), interchanges, bamboo gardens — keyword-matched but not curated by 'iconic-view' criteria. Hokusai-specific viewpoints (Kanagawa-oki, Lake Kawaguchi reverse-Fuji) not prioritized.

reasoning: User asked about 'lesser-known UNESCO sites' but tool returns Japan Heritage stories (Bunka-cho program), not UNESCO World Heritage. These are different designations — risk of conflating them. Data itself is fine but mismatched to intent.
missing_data: Actual UNESCO World Heritage list (cultural+natural), tentative-list sites.
wrong_data: Japan Heritage ≠ UNESCO; presenting them under a UNESCO query risks grounding error downstream.
improvement_hint: Add a separate get_unesco_sites tool, or label clearly that Japan Heritage is a domestic designation distinct from UNESCO.

reasoning: Joumon query returns Sannai-Maruyama-area museums (Aomori) and Nagano Hoshikuso-toge experiences. Genuinely on-topic. Some Nara-tourism boilerplate noise. The real Joumon culture areas in northern Tohoku/Aomori UNESCO sites are partially surfaced.

improvement_hint: Cross-link to UNESCO Jomon site list; suppress generic prefectural tourism portals lacking Joumon-specific content.

reasoning: User specifically asks about fermented food culture (发酵食品). Tool dumps full MAFF GI list (507 items) without category filter for fermentation. Top items (cassis, Tajima beef, Kobe beef, Yubari melon) are not fermented foods. Lang=zh honored well.
missing_data: Category filter for fermented foods (miso, soy sauce, sake, natto, narezushi, tsukemono).
improvement_hint: Add a fermented-food subcategory or keyword filter (発酵/醸造) on get_local_food.

reasoning: Query is shamanic mountain traditions (Itako, Yamabushi, Shugendo). Tool returned Nogaku and miscellaneous folk festivals — Kawagoe Hikawa, Yobuko, Okumakabuto. None directly on shamanism. Dewa Sanzan / Osorezan / Yoshino-Omine not surfaced.

reasoning: METI Densan craft list with production area, designation date, prefecture — exactly what's needed to identify single-craft towns (Nibutani Attus → Biratori; Oitama → Yonezawa; Tsuruoka shina cloth). 231 items, well structured.
improvement_hint: Could add 'craft town concentration' metric (e.g. % of municipality employment in craft) to flag mono-craft towns.

reasoning: Solid hits: Tomioka Silk Mill (UNESCO), Kagoshima Shuseikan (Meiji industrial revolution sites), Nagoya industrial tourism, Aizu Wakamatsu programs. Directly answers query about preserved industrial-decline response.

reasoning: 81 festivals returned, all ICH-designated, well-grounded. But intent is 'dying / endangered festivals in remote areas' — no endangered-status flag. Many listed are still well-attended major festivals (Kawagoe Hikawa, Tatemon).
